chunk_summary: This section focuses on neurodevelopmental screening tools and psychosocial support strategies for stunted children, emphasizing validated tools and global guidelines for early intervention in chronic undernutrition cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of stunting on neurodevelopment and psychosocial health.
Learn about validated screening tools like the Ages and Stages Questionnaire and Trivandrum Developmental Screening Chart.
Explore global frameworks such as the WHO Care for Child Development package for intervention.
Identify strategies for integrating psychosocial support into care for stunted children.
key_facts: Stunting affects approximately 22% of children under 5 globally, impairing neurodevelopment and psychosocial health.
The Ages and Stages Questionnaire is a validated tool for developmental screening in Indian children (Indian Pediatrics, 2012).
The Trivandrum Developmental Screening Chart was developed and validated for early detection of developmental delays (PubMed ID: 9685454).
The WHO Care for Child Development package provides evidence-based guidelines for psychosocial support in undernourished children.
UNICEF emphasizes the importance of integrating psychosocial care into child development programs in resource-limited settings.
reference_quote: The WHO Care for Child Development package provides practical guidance for improving psychosocial stimulation and caregiver-child interactions in undernourished children (WHO, 2007).
document_key_topics: Neurodevelopmental screening tools for stunted children
Psychosocial support strategies in chronic undernutrition
Global guidelines for early intervention in stunting
Validation of developmental screening tools in resource-limited settings
